user_prompt:
Clean technical schematic of an fNIRS prefrontal optode montage. TWO-PANEL layout side by side. Publication-quality, IEEE/Nature paper aesthetic. White background. Sans-serif labels. NO photorealism, NO 3D rendering, NO real human face.

LEFT PANEL ('Anatomical placement'): top-down view of a stylised prefrontal scalp area (simple oval outline representing forehead, with reference points at Fp1/Fp2/Fpz). On this oval, place 8 SOURCES as filled black circles labeled S1, S2, S3, S4, S5, S6, S7, S8 distributed across the prefrontal area. Place 8 DETECTORS as open circles labeled D1, D2, D3, D4, D5, D6, D7, D8 interleaved between sources. Draw light grey lines indicating the 23 source-detector channel pairs that comprise the montage.

RIGHT PANEL ('5x7 spatial grid mapping'): a clean 5-row x 7-column rectangular grid. Place 23 small filled circles at the following grid coordinates (row, col) labeled with the channel name:
S1_D1 at (0,2); S1_D3 at (1,1); S2_D2 at (0,4); S2_D1 at (0,3); S2_D5 at (1,4); S3_D1 at (1,2); S3_D3 at (2,1); S3_D4 at (2,2); S3_D6 at (3,1); S4_D4 at (2,3); S4_D5 at (2,4); S4_D7 at (3,4); S5_D2 at (1,5); S5_D5 at (2,5); S5_D8 at (3,6); S6_D3 at (3,0); S6_D6 at (4,1); S7_D4 at (3,2); S7_D6 at (4,2); S7_D7 at (4,3); S8_D5 at (3,5); S8_D7 at (4,4); S8_D8 at (4,5).
Empty grid cells stay empty (no dot).

Connect the two panels with a dashed arrow labeled 'spatial encoding'.

Caption beneath both panels: 'Prefrontal montage: 8 sources, 8 detectors, 23 channels; sampling 10.17 Hz; mapped to a 5x7 spatial grid for downstream graph construction'.

Avoid: realistic brain anatomy, photographic skin texture, MRI overlays, decorative icons. Use only thin black lines and small circular markers.

iterations.prompt:
Create a high-quality scientific diagram with these requirements:

VISUAL QUALITY:
- Clean white or light background (no textures or gradients)
- High contrast for readability and printing
- Professional, publication-ready appearance
- Sharp, clear lines and text
- Adequate spacing between elements to prevent crowding

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Clear, readable sans-serif fonts (Arial, Helvetica style)
- Minimum 10pt font size for all labels
- Consistent font sizes throughout
- All text horizontal or clearly readable
- No overlapping text

SCIENTIFIC STANDARDS:
- Accurate representation of concepts
- Clear labels for all components
- Include scale bars, legends, or axes where appropriate
- Use standard scientific notation and symbols
- Include units where applicable

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Colorblind-friendly color palette (use Okabe-Ito colors if using color)
- High contrast between elements
- Redundant encoding (shapes + colors, not just colors)
- Works well in grayscale

LAYOUT:
- Logical flow (left-to-right or top-to-bottom)
- Clear visual hierarchy
- Balanced composition
- Appropriate use of whitespace
- No clutter or unnecessary decorative elements

IMPORTANT - NO FIGURE NUMBERS:
- Do NOT include "Figure 1:", "Fig. 1", or any figure numbering in the image
- Do NOT add captions or titles like "Figure: ..." at the top or bottom
- Figure numbers and captions are added separately in the document/LaTeX
- The diagram should contain only the visual content itself


USER REQUEST: Clean technical schematic of an fNIRS prefrontal optode montage. TWO-PANEL layout side by side. Publication-quality, IEEE/Nature paper aesthetic. White background. Sans-serif labels. NO photorealism, NO 3D rendering, NO real human face.

Place 8 DETECTORS as open circles labeled D1, D2, D3, D4, D5, D6, D7, D8 interleaved between sources. Draw light grey lines indicating the 23 source-detector channel pairs that comprise the montage.

Avoid: realistic brain anatomy, photographic skin texture, MRI overlays, decorative icons. Use only thin black lines and small circular markers.

Generate a publication-quality scientific diagram that meets all the guidelines above.

early_stop_reason: Quality score 7.5 meets threshold 8.5 for journal
